one_time_description: Additional COVID-19 funding received from Indigenous Services Canada for public health measures and reopening costs
economic_notes: Nation has investments in Pimee Well Servicing Ltd. (17% ownership), Pimee Limited Partnership, Seven Lakes Oilfield Services Limited Partnership (8.33%), Akuna Drilling Trust, 1678902 Alberta Ltd. (100%), and Beaver Lake Cree Nation Holdings GP Inc. (100%). Oil and gas royalties and surface rights payments administered by Government of Canada under Indian Oil and Gas Act. Large infrastructure project under construction ($10,638,730).
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2022 (not 2024). Consolidated entity includes Beaver Lake Education Authority (Amisk Community School), Beaver Lake Wah-Pow Treatment Centre, Beaver Lake Health Centre, and Beaver Lake Government and Industry Relations. Two GBEs accounted for by equity method: Pimee Well Servicing Ltd. and Seven Lakes Oilfield Services Limited Partnership. Significant COVID-19 additional funding of $7,316,744 received from ISC inflated revenue substantially versus prior year. Large deferred revenue balance of $15.5M primarily from ISC programs. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($16,079,688) + ISC Health Canada ($2,426,283) + CMHC ($140,079) + HRD Canada ($582,624) = $19,228,674. OSR calculated as total revenue minus government transfers. No schedule of remuneration included in the statements provided. Expenses_infrastructure mapped to Public Works segment; expenses_enterprises mapped to Economic Development segment; expenses_administration mapped to Band Government segment.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
